name: secret-guard
description: PreToolUse(Bash) ADVISORY hook enforcing commons @D g77 (credential exists-check before register / login), implemented in hexa-lang (`_secret_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Seals the recurring 'credential is missing / please register' anti-pattern when the key ACTUALLY EXISTS in the `secret` store (the 2026-05-30 HF token incident: an agent saw a `huggingface.token` 401 and told the user to register, but `hf.token` held a valid token). Two detectors run on a Bash command, each consulting `secret list` (key NAMES only — values are NEVER read or printed): (a) `secret set <K>` where <K> already exists in `secret list` → advises it already exists, read via `secret get <K>`, overwrite only if rotating; (b) credential-acquisition commands — `hf auth login` · `huggingface-cli login` · `gh auth login` · `aws configure` · `<tool> login` — when a plausibly-corresponding key EXISTS in `secret list` (service→key mapped heuristically: hf/huggingface → keys matching `hf`/`hugg`; gh/github → `github`/`gh`; aws → `aws`), surfacing the matching key names plus a known-alias hint (huggingface.token ↔ hf.token) so the agent resolves via `secret get <key>` before re-registering. ADVISORY ONLY — emits `permissionDecision: "allow"` with an `additionalContext` string, NEVER deny (commons @D g5 / guards-narrow-scope: must not block legitimate re-registration / rotation); mirrors git-guard's non-blocking advisory emit shape. CONSERVATIVE — fires only on a real match (a real `secret set`/login AND a matching existing key); `secret list` / `secret get` / `--help` / bare reads pass SILENTLY. Shell separators (; & | () ` " ' \) normalized to whitespace before tokenizing so `cd /x && secret set K v`, env-prefixes, `bash -c '… gh auth login …'` are seen. FAILS OPEN on any error (no advisory, never block). NO opt-out by design — no env var, no config file, no exception list (project.tape @D s11).
